# mermaid-azure-icons — Copilot and Claude context

This file is auto-read by GitHub Copilot for all sessions in this workspace.
It is also the system prompt to use with Claude when generating architecture diagrams.
The slug tables below are auto-regenerated by `npm run build:icons` — do not edit manually.

## Repository purpose

`mermaid-azure-icons` converts official Microsoft Azure and Fabric SVG icons into
Mermaid-compatible Iconify JSON packs. It enables `architecture-beta` Mermaid diagrams
with real Microsoft service icons.

## Build pipeline

1. `scripts/fetch.ts` — downloads latest SVGs from upstream into `raw/`
2. `scripts/convert.ts` — converts SVGs to Iconify JSON with gradient ID namespacing
3. `scripts/build.ts` — assembles `dist/fabric.json` and `dist/azure.json`, regenerates
   this file's slug tables and `docs/icon-catalogue.md`
4. `src/index.ts` — exports `registerFabricIcons()` and `registerAzureIcons()` helpers

## Critical rule: gradient ID namespacing

Fabric SVGs use `fill="url(#id)"`. When inlined into one JSON, IDs from different icons
collide. `scripts/utils/namespace-ids.ts` prefixes every id/url()/href() value with the
icon slug. Example: slug `lakehouse`, id `a` → `fabric-lakehouse-a` everywhere in that body.

## Iconify JSON format (what dist/fabric.json and dist/azure.json look like)

```json
{
  "prefix": "fabric",
  "icons": {
    "lakehouse": {
      "body": "......",
      "width": 48,
      "height": 48
    }
  }
}
```

## Mermaid architecture-beta syntax
```
architecture-beta
group ws(azure:microsoft-fabric)[Workspace label]
service lh(fabric:lakehouse)[Label] in ws
service es(fabric:eventstream)[Label] in ws
service eh(fabric:eventhouse)[Label] in ws
lh:R --> L:es
es:R --> L:eh

```
Arrow sides: L (left), R (right), T (top), B (bottom)
Arrow types: `-->` line with arrow, `` bidirectional

## Diagram generation rules

When generating an architecture diagram:
1. Always use `architecture-beta` diagram type, not `flowchart`
2. Use `fabric:` prefix for Fabric services, `azure:` prefix for Azure services
3. Group related services with `group` blocks
4. Use only slugs from the tables below — never invent slugs
5. If unsure of a slug, use `(server)` as the built-in fallback icon
6. Remind the user to call registerFabricIcons(mermaid) / registerAzureIcons(mermaid) first
